program.name: Coles Group Graduate Program
program.open_date_raw: Applications will be open from 10 March to 12 April 2026 for our February 2027 intake.
program.close_date_raw: Applications will be open from 10 March to 12 April 2026 for our February 2027 intake.
program.rotation_text: The program includes rotations across different teams (rotation options vary by stream).

eligibility.work_rights: Australian or New Zealand citizens or permanent residents at the time of application can apply.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting the program (for this intake, before the program starts in February 2027).
minimum_requirements: You must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or a permanent resident, at the time you apply.
You must have completed a bachelor’s degree before the program starts.

stages.name: Online assessment and video interview
stages.details: Complete an online assessment that includes a video interview. It is not timed and should take about 45 minutes in total.

stages.name: Discovery Centre
stages.details: Attend a Discovery Centre and take part in activities. Every candidate who participates will receive feedback.

stages.name: Verbal offer
stages.details: If successful, you will receive a verbal offer. After this, you complete pre-hire checks, accept your contract, complete online onboarding, and then start the program.

tips: Register your interest to be notified when applications open.
Expect the online assessment (including video interview) to take around 45 minutes.
If you attend a Discovery Centre, you will receive feedback whether you are successful or not.

exec_themes: Keeping grocery prices competitive and focusing on value for customers (including growing Exclusive to Coles / own-brand range).
Growing digital and omnichannel shopping (eCommerce growth; online + stores).
Store network investment (new stores and renewals) and improving operational efficiency (including energy efficiency upgrades).
Integrating sustainability into core business strategy, with focus areas of climate, nature, and circularity.
Strengthening responsible sourcing and human rights practices across the supply chain, and supporting communities (food donations, grants).
commercial_context.esg: Coles’ FY26–FY30 Sustainability Strategy is organised around three pillars: climate, nature and circularity. It is supported by ongoing commitments to human rights, enabling and developing teams, diversity and inclusion, health/safety/wellbeing, community support, and healthy/safe/affordable food. The FY25 Sustainability Report also states targets and progress, including maintaining 100% renewable electricity sourcing for operations and emissions reduction targets (Scope 1 and 2), plus waste diversion/packaging initiatives and ethical sourcing work.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Coles describes a refreshed FY26–FY30 Sustainability Strategy focused on three pillars—climate, nature, and circularity—positioned as a shift/refocus in how it prioritises sustainability efforts. Coles also reports achieving 100% renewable electricity sourcing for its operations (a milestone reached in FY25, reported publicly on 26 August 2025).

notes: Program page states the application window (10 March to 12 April 2026) and that it is for the February 2027 intake.
Program page lists example graduate streams and describes offices nationwide and travel to Melbourne Store Support Centre.
Graduate streams page lists the program length as 2 years and provides rotation possibilities by stream.
Eligibility section on the Graduate Program page states citizenship/permanent residency requirement and that you must have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting.
The First Nations Graduate Program page URL found in search results returned a 404 when opened, so no additional eligibility details were extracted from it.
Graduate Program page describes a four-step process and provides extra detail on the online assessment/video interview duration, Discovery Centre feedback, and the post-offer steps.

The 2025 Annual Report PDF could not be accessed due to a 'System Unavailable' error on the colesgroup.com.au file endpoint during retrieval. Because of this, 'profit_engine' and 'headwinds' are set to null (they are typically spelled out in the Operating and Financial Review / risk sections of the Annual Report and/or results materials).

Exec themes were inferred from the official 'Reports' page FY25 highlights (eCommerce growth, Exclusive to Coles products, store renewals) plus the FY25 Sustainability Report’s stated strategy pillars/commitments. These are themes visible in official materials, but not a complete management discussion.
